title: Conflict Diagnosis and Semantic Resolution · History Workshop
summary: Resolve an integration conflict by reconstructing both intents and verifying behavior rather than deleting markers until Git accepts the file. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a conflict reproduction, resolution, and regression record while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inspect unmerged index stages, ours/theirs/base terminology, paths, and operation context before editing.
Read conflict markers and diff views while obtaining surrounding base and commit intent.
Construct a new result from requirements, tests, and both histories rather than treating resolution as text selection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Merge conflict in policy.txt has base limit=2, main limit=2, and repair limit=3 plus retry tests. Inspect index stages, reconstruct intent, create a semantic result, and verify before continue.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inspect unmerged index stages, ours/theirs/base terminology, paths, and operation context before editing.

content.text: Merge conflict in policy.txt has base limit=2, main limit=2, and repair limit=3 plus retry tests. Inspect index stages, reconstruct intent, create a semantic result, and verify before continue.

content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Conflict Stages condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Pro Git, Second Edition — Controls cumulative conceptual sequence from setup through internals, collaboration, tooling, and distributed workflows.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-rerere-regression-16xts1" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Rerere Regression into observable performance evidence.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write the workshop evidence note for Rerere Regression: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Merge conflict in policy.txt has base limit=2, main limit=6, and repair limit=7 plus retry tests. Inspect index stages, reconstruct intent, create a semantic result, and verify before continue.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record or reuse conflict resolutions only after verifying context, tests, and changed-case behavior.
